scenario.name: Strait of Hormuz Crisis — April 2026
scenario.description: Eight weeks into the US-Israeli war on Iran. The Apr 7 ceasefire was extended indefinitely by Trump on Apr 21 but Iran calls it 'meaningless' while the US naval blockade (since Apr 13) continues. Islamabad Talks failed; Trump canceled the Witkoff/Kushner second round on Apr 25. Three US carrier strike groups in theater. Iran is seizing commercial ships; US is seizing Iranian tankers. Brent above $105 (+16-18% on the week). What happens next?
scenario.background: Eight weeks into the 2026 Iran war. The US-Iran ceasefire signed April 7 took effect Apr 8 but Israel immediately launched 'Operation Eternal Darkness' on Hezbollah — 100 strikes in 10 minutes — and the Strait remained functionally closed under Iranian $1M+ tolls. Islamabad Talks (Apr 11-12) collapsed after 21 hours over Iran's nuclear program, the Strait, sanctions, and $6B in frozen assets. Trump declared a full US naval blockade of Iran on Apr 13. The blockade is enforced by the Fifth Fleet with three carrier strike groups (Ford, Lincoln, Bush) and ~10 destroyers — first time in decades. Iran has retaliated by seizing the container ships Epaminondas and MSC Francesca (Apr 22) plus two more vessels on Apr 25; the US has seized the Iran-flagged Touska and tankers Deep Sea/Dorena/Sevin/Derya. Trump extended the ceasefire indefinitely on Apr 21 but kept the blockade; Iran called it 'meaningless.' Trump's 'wide-scale destruction' threat sits in reserve. On Apr 24 the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire was extended 3 weeks but is violated daily. On Apr 25 Trump canceled the Witkoff/Kushner Islamabad trip ('Iran offered a lot, but not enough'). The negotiation track is suspended. Brent is at ~$105 (up 16-18% on the week); WTI at ~$94 with the Brent-WTI spread blown out. Saudi Arabia's East-West Pipeline is back to its full ~7M bpd capacity — but the Yanbu Red Sea terminal is now the bottleneck. ~20,000 mariners and 2,000 ships are stranded per IMO.

zh.name: 霍尔木兹海峡危机 — 2026年4月
briefing: 美以对伊朗开战第八周。停火无限期延长但伊朗称'毫无意义'。
美军海上封锁继续；3个航母战斗群 + 10艘驱逐舰在位。
伊斯兰堡谈判破裂；特朗普4/25取消第二轮特使行程。
美伊互相扣船。布伦特$105，周线+16-18%。下一步呢？

en.name: Strait of Hormuz Crisis — April 2026
briefing: Week 8 of the US-Israeli war on Iran. Ceasefire extended indefinitely but Iran calls it 'meaningless.'
US naval blockade in force. Three carrier strike groups + 10 destroyers in CENTCOM.
Islamabad Talks failed. Trump canceled Witkoff/Kushner Apr 25.
Both sides seizing ships. Brent at $105, +16-18% on the week. What now?

market_agent.system_prompt:
You are a senior oil market analyst. You are tracking the ongoing 2026 Iran War and Strait of Hormuz crisis that began February 28, 2026.

Current oil market context (as of April 26, 2026):
- Brent crude at ~$104-106/bbl (Apr 24 settle ~$104.4; intraday $106) — gained ~16-18% on the week as Islamabad talks collapsed
- WTI at ~$94/bbl (June contract Apr 24 ~$94.40) — Brent-WTI spread blown out to ~$10 reflecting regional risk premium
- Gold at ~$4,740/oz (Apr 24 close), continued safe-haven bid
- US-Iran ceasefire (signed Apr 7) extended INDEFINITELY by Trump on Apr 21 — Iran calls extension 'meaningless' while US naval blockade continues
- Islamabad Talks (Apr 11-12) failed after 21 hours; Trump CANCELED Witkoff/Kushner second-round trip Apr 25 ('Iran offered a lot, but not enough')
- US naval blockade of Iran in effect since Apr 13; THREE US carrier strike groups now in CENTCOM (Ford, Lincoln, Bush) + ~10 destroyers
- Iran retaliating by seizing commercial vessels (Epaminondas, MSC Francesca, plus 2 more on Apr 25); US has seized Iran-flagged Touska + tankers Deep Sea/Dorena/Sevin/Derya
- Strait of Hormuz traffic back to March-war lows; ~20,000 mariners and 2,000 ships stranded per IMO
- Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 3 weeks Apr 24 but violated daily (Hezbollah not party)
- ~13M bbl/day of Middle East output offline due to Hormuz; Saudi East-West Pipeline RESTORED to full ~7M bpd capacity Apr 12-13 (Yanbu terminal now the bottleneck at ~3-5M bpd)
- OPEC+ Apr 5 agreed to +206 kbpd hike but largely 'on paper' — Iraq (95% Hormuz-dependent, ~4M bpd), Kuwait, UAE physically cannot deliver
- Spare capacity ~5 mb/d nominal: Saudi 3 / UAE 1 / Kuwait 0.4 — stranded behind the chokepoint
- War-risk insurance premiums for tankers at 10x+ pre-crisis levels
- US-China trade war ongoing (36% average tariff on Chinese goods)
- Trump's Apr 22 '3-5 day' ultimatum has lapsed; return to 'wide-scale destruction' strike posture is the live alternative
- Goldman Sachs: Brent stays above $100 throughout 2026 if disruption extends another month

After each round of actions, update the oil price and escalation index. Pricing framework:
- Oil reacts to escalation: P_new = P_old * (1 + alpha * delta_escalation + noise)
  alpha ~ 0.05-0.20 depending on severity of supply impact
- De-escalation can reduce prices, but risk premium remains sticky
- escalation_index (0.0-1.0) reflects overall war intensity and supply disruption risk
- Consider: Strait status, Houthi Red Sea threat, Saudi spare capacity, infrastructure damage, ceasefire viability, blockade enforcement, ship seizures
- Current baseline: stalemate w/ blockade = ~$105; credible talks restart = ~$90; US strikes resume = $120+; full Hormuz re-closure = $130+

Be specific: VLCC insurance, contango/backwardation, SPR releases, freight rates, refinery margins.
